sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Southland Holdings (SLND) shifts $110M term debt to sureties and gains waivers
feed.summary: Southland Holdings (SLND) has restructured approximately $110 million of its term loan facilities, transferring this debt to its surety providers. The sureties have waived quarterly principal and monthly interest payments until maturity, along with existing defaults and covenant violations, in exchange for Southland agreeing to sell idle equipment and use proceeds to reduce principal. This move, while providing immediate relief, highlights the company's financial strain and reliance on its sureties for continued operations and future financing.

feed.title: LEXI Stock Price, News & Analysis | Alexis Practical Tactical ETF
feed.summary: The Alexis Practical Tactical ETF (LEXI) is an exchange-traded fund managed by Alexis Investment Partners, LLC, which trades on the Nasdaq exchange. The ETF employs a tactical allocation strategy, allowing it to adjust its exposure across various asset classes, including stocks, bonds, gold, and REITs, in response to market conditions. LEXI is benchmarked against a blend of the Morningstar Global Markets Index, S&P 500 Index, and Bloomberg US Aggregate Bond Index, and has received a 5-star Morningstar Rating in the Tactical Allocation category.

feed.title: The Case for Darden Restaurants (DRI) Gets Stronger
feed.summary: Darden Restaurants (DRI) is showing strengthening performance, leading analysts to raise price targets and maintain positive ratings. Following its Q3 FY 2026 results, BofA increased its price target to $272, citing improved sales projections and lower commodity inflation, while Bernstein expects a nearly 3.5% price rise with minimal impact on traffic, especially for LongHorn. Despite this, the article suggests that some AI stocks might offer greater returns in a shorter timeframe.

feed.title: FERC sets new ROE for New England transmission owners, PPL evaluates response
feed.summary: The Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) has issued Opinion No. 594, setting a new methodology for determining the return on equity (ROE) for New England transmission owners (NETOs), including a base ROE of 9.57% and a maximum of 12.09% with incentives. This decision is retroactive to October 16, 2014, and requires refunds. PPL Corporation, whose subsidiary Rhode Island Energy is affected, is reviewing the order but does not expect a material impact on its operations or financial condition, reaffirming its 2026 forecast and long-term growth targets.

feed.title: Trimble senior vice president Peter Large announces retirement effective May
feed.summary: Peter Large, Trimble's Senior Vice President overseeing Strategy, Corporate Development, Corporate Partnerships and Alliances, and the Office of Technology Innovation, announced his retirement effective late May 2026. His decision was not due to disagreements with the company and he will not receive severance, but his retirement qualifies under Trimble's Age & Service Equity Vesting Program. This news follows a strong Q4 2025 for Trimble, with earnings surpassing analyst expectations, and positive analyst ratings from Bernstein SocGen Group and Oppenheimer.

feed.title: Assessing Becton Dickinson (BDX) Valuation After Recent Share Price Weakness And Business Separation Plans
feed.summary: Becton Dickinson (BDX) has seen its share price decline by approximately 16% over the past month and 20% in the last three months, trading at $156.23. Despite this weakness, a common narrative suggests the stock is undervalued with a fair value of $197.15, supported by the anticipated separation of its Biosciences and Diagnostic Solutions business, expected to improve cash flow and margins. However, investors are advised to consider potential risks like trade headwinds and the complexity of the business separation before making investment decisions.

feed.title: LSI Industries Closes Royston Acquisition and New Credit Facility
feed.summary: LSI Industries (LYTS) has successfully closed its acquisition of Royston for $325 million and secured a new senior credit facility of up to $350 million. This strategic move aims to expand LSI's integrated retail-branding platform, bolstering its Display Solutions segment and positioning it as a comprehensive provider for North American retail build-out projects. Analysts currently rate LYTS as a Buy with a $30.00 price target, and TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, also rates it as Outperform, driven by strong fundamentals and a positive earnings outlook.

feed.title: Seagate (STX) jumps as bullish analyst target hikes and strong AI-storage outlook keep momentum intact
feed.summary: Seagate Technology Holdings plc (STX) stock rose by 6.9% due to continued bullish sentiment, strong recent results, and an optimistic March-quarter outlook. Analyst price targets have increased, driven by anticipated AI/data-center storage demand and the company's HAMR technology ramp. Insider trading shows 141 sales by executives and no purchases in the last six months, while institutional investors have both added and decreased positions.

feed.title: Alphabet Inc. (Class C) stock slides over 1.5% amid tech sector pressure and geopolitical tensions
feed.summary: Alphabet Inc. (Class C) stock, ticker GOOG, fell over 1.5% in early Nasdaq trading on March 24, 2026, dropping to around $293 amid broader tech sector weakness driven by Middle East geopolitical tensions and rising oil prices. Despite this short-term pullback, analysts remain strongly bullish on Alphabet, with 86% rating it a "Buy" due to its AI monetization and cloud growth fundamentals. For US investors, the dip is seen as a potential accumulation opportunity, underpinned by strong long-term prospects and analyst consensus.

feed.title: Innodata Expands Wells Fargo Revolving Credit Facility
feed.summary: Innodata (INOD) has expanded and extended its revolving credit facility with Wells Fargo to up to $50 million, with a new maturity date of April 4, 2029. This strategic move aims to provide Innodata with increased financial flexibility to support anticipated growth and rising accounts receivable. The facility's borrowing base, which was approximately $30 million as of December 31, 2025, is expected to grow with the company's anticipated increases in accounts receivable.

feed.title: PPL Corp - FERC issues opinion no. 594 on ROE for New England transmission owners - SEC filing
feed.summary: PPL Corp announced that the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) has issued opinion number 594 regarding the Return on Equity (ROE) for New England transmission owners. This news, disclosed via an SEC filing, is significant for PPL Corporation, which is involved in electricity generation, transmission, and distribution across Kentucky, Pennsylvania, and Rhode Island. The article also lists recent analyst adjustments to PPL's price target.

feed.title: Yum! Brands Inc stock faces horizontal trend amid analyst Moderate Buy consensus and recent institutional moves
feed.summary: Yum! Brands Inc. stock is experiencing a horizontal trading trend on the NYSE, characterized by controlled movements and recent institutional activity. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with price targets up to $185, despite a recent Q4 EPS miss. The company's raised quarterly dividend and resilient franchised model in the consumer staples sector continue to attract income-oriented US investors.

feed.title: Liquidia Stock Up 135% as Insider Sells $1.3 Million in Shares
feed.summary: Liquidia's Chief Medical Officer, Rajeev Saggar, sold 35,365 shares worth $1.28 million, a sale significantly larger than his previous transactions. This insider sale comes as Liquidia's stock has surged 135% in the last year, driven by strong product sales and profitability from its YUTREPIA launch. Despite a net loss for the full year due to scaling efforts, the company maintains a strong cash position, and the transaction is viewed as an insider locking in gains rather than a sign of weakness.

feed.title: LSI Industries completes $325M Royston Group acquisition
feed.summary: LSI Industries Inc. (NASDAQ:LYTS) has completed its acquisition of Royston Group for $325 million, comprising cash and LSI common stock. This strategic move, financed through debt and a public stock offering, significantly expands LSI's retail solutions platform and increases its debt-to-equity ratio to 1.75. The acquisition is expected to integrate Royston's financial results into LSI’s Display Solutions segment starting in fiscal 2026 third quarter, following Royston's generation of approximately $272 million in revenue and $38 million in adjusted EBITDA for the twelve months ending September 30, 2025.

feed.title: Li Bang International plans 1-for-100 reverse stock split
feed.summary: Li Bang International Corporation Inc. (NASDAQ:LBGJ) announced a 1-for-100 reverse stock split of its ordinary shares, effective upon market open on Thursday, to regain compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement. The company's stock currently trades at $0.02, having dropped 98% in the past six months. This action, approved by shareholders and the board, will consolidate every 100 existing shares into one, without altering shareholders' percentage interest except for fractional share adjustments.

feed.title: Univest Securities, LLC Announces Closing of $16 Million Best Efforts Public Offering for its Client Linkers Industries Limited (NASDAQ: LNKS)
feed.summary: Univest Securities, LLC announced the closing of a $16 million best efforts public offering for its client Linkers Industries Limited (NASDAQ: LNKS). The offering included 23,088,023 units, each comprised of Class A ordinary shares (or pre-funded warrants) and Series A and B Warrants. Linkers Industries Limited plans to use the net proceeds for a potential acquisition of additional equity in LPW Electronics Co Ltd, capital expenditures, operating capacity, working capital, and future business expansions.

feed.title: Travelers Dismisses Insurance Suit Over YouTube Addiction Claims
feed.summary: A Travelers Companies insurer, St. Paul Fire & Marine Insurance Co., has voluntarily dismissed its lawsuit against Alphabet Inc.'s Google concerning coverage for claims that YouTube's algorithms were designed to addict children. While this specific suit in Delaware has been dropped, parallel legal actions involving Google and its insurers, including a case in California and another in Delaware against AIG units, are still ongoing. This dismissal is part of broader litigation concerning social media addiction against tech companies.

feed.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of Insulet ...
feed.summary: Pomerantz LLP is investigating potential securities fraud claims against Insulet Corporation (NASDAQ: PODD) on behalf of its investors. This investigation follows Insulet's disclosure on March 12, 2026, that it initiated a voluntary Medical Device Correction for specific lots of its Omnipod® 5 Pods due to a manufacturing issue. Following this announcement, Insulet's stock price dropped significantly.

feed.title: Spectral AI Inc. (NASDAQ:MDAI) Reports Q4 Revenue Beat but Wider Loss, Stock Gains on Strong Cash Position
feed.summary: Spectral AI Inc. (NASDAQ:MDAI) released its Q4 2025 financial results, reporting a revenue beat of $3.85 million against an estimate of $2.98 million, but a wider non-GAAP loss per share of $0.11. Despite the loss, the stock saw gains in after-hours trading, driven by the revenue beat and a significantly strengthened cash position of $15.4 million. The company's focus remains on regulatory progress for its DeepView® System and its BARDA contract, with management providing a confident revenue guidance of $18.5 million for 2026, surpassing analyst estimates.

feed.title: Crown Castle Inc. stock faces pressure from growth concerns and analyst downgrade amid fiber unit sale plans
feed.summary: Crown Castle Inc. (CCI) stock is experiencing significant pressure due to growth concerns and an analyst downgrade from Wells Fargo, despite plans to sell its fiber business. The company, a leading US tower operator, aims to refocus on its core tower assets and strengthen its balance sheet, but analysts question if this will fully offset decelerating growth in tower leasing amid maturing 5G deployments. US investors are left to weigh a potential entry point against ongoing risks like carrier capital expenditure restraint and high debt.

feed.title: Imunon Expands At-The-Market Equity Offering Capacity
feed.summary: Imunon (IMNN) has expanded its at-the-market (ATM) equity offering capacity to $17 million by registering an additional $7 million of common stock with H.C. Wainwright & Co. This move indicates the company's ongoing reliance on public equity markets for funding, which could lead to future shareholder dilution. While an analyst has a Buy rating on IMNN with a $25.00 price target, TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates it Neutral due to weak financial performance and technical downtrends, despite positive earnings call updates.

feed.title: Parker-Hannifin stock faces institutional trimming amid strong aerospace backlog and analyst upgrade
feed.summary: Parker-Hannifin is seeing institutional position adjustments, with Congress Asset Management slightly reducing its stake while others like Ameriflex Group increased theirs, and Rep. David Taylor also purchased shares. Despite some insider selling, the company's strong aerospace backlog, recent acquisitions, and analyst upgrades to a "Moderate Buy" consensus indicate robust fundamentals and potential for continued growth, particularly in the industrial and aerospace sectors. The stock has significantly outperformed the S&P 500 over the past decade, driven by strategic market positioning and dividend payments.

feed.title: Weyerhaeuser Wood Products Face Supply Chain Pressures Amid 2026 Lumber Market Shifts
feed.summary: Weyerhaeuser's wood products division is experiencing softer demand and elevated production costs in early 2026 due to high interest rates and volatile commodity prices, impacting the US housing and construction sectors. Despite these pressures, the company is strategically drawing down inventory, innovating with engineered lumber, and leveraging sustainable practices and vertical integration to mitigate risks and position for a potential market rebound. Analysts rate the stock as "Hold," with future recovery dependent on easing interest rates, infrastructure spending, and continued demand for sustainable wood solutions.

feed.title: Pacific Biosciences of California researcher uncovers genetic variants shaping brain disorders
feed.summary: Pacific Biosciences of California is exploring the genetic basis of rare ataxias to understand neurological function and disorders better. By focusing on small communities, the company's research aims to identify subtle genetic variants and population patterns to advance personalized medicine and neurological research. This initiative builds on their recent developments in HiFi sequencing technology for detailed cancer variation analysis and automatic epigenetic detection.

feed.title: AM Best Comments on Credit Ratings of The Wawanesa Mutual Insurance Company Following Announced Acquisition of Everest Insurance Company of Canada
feed.summary: AM Best has affirmed that the Credit Ratings of The Wawanesa Mutual Insurance Company remain unchanged following its announced acquisition of Everest Insurance Company of Canada. This acquisition is expected to enhance Wawanesa Mutual’s commercial insurance business by diversifying its portfolio with new specialty and financial lines, adding approximately $305 million in direct premiums. The transaction is anticipated to close in Q4 2026, subject to regulatory approvals, and is not expected to significantly impact Wawanesa Mutual's balance sheet or operating performance in the near term.

feed.title: Fathom Holdings Adds Subordinated Bridge Note Financing
feed.summary: Fathom Holdings (FTHM) secured a $2 million subordinated secured bridge promissory note on March 18, 2026, with an April 1, 2027 maturity and 9% interest. While this financing boosts short-term liquidity, it introduces tighter operating constraints, increased leverage, and stronger creditor control due to extensive covenants and a secured lien on assets. Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, rates FTHM as Neutral, citing weak financial quality and challenging valuation despite encouraging growth in the latest earnings call.

feed.title: Should DuPont’s (DD) Discretionary Reverse Split Authority Reframe How Investors View Its Earnings Stability Narrative?
feed.summary: DuPont recently announced it is seeking shareholder approval for a discretionary reverse stock split, which could adjust its share count and price structure. While this is primarily a technical move, it may influence how investors perceive DuPont's capital structure alongside ongoing portfolio changes and legal considerations, particularly concerning earnings stability and PFAS liabilities. The company's future revenue and earnings projections, potential fair value upside, and unresolved litigation are key factors for investors to consider.

feed.title: Oil Prices Dive. Trump Says Iran Has Agreed to No Nuclear Weapons.
feed.summary: Chevron CEO Mike Wirth believes energy markets are not accurately pricing the oil supply shock caused by the ongoing war in Iran. Speaking at the CERAWeek conference, Wirth indicated that crude futures should be higher given the significant supply reduction and extensive infrastructure damage. A record number of attendees are present at this year's annual gathering in Houston.

feed.title: Global Payments Drop More Than 28% in One Year: What’s the Best Way to Approach This Stock?
feed.summary: Global Payments Inc. (GPN) has seen its stock drop over 28% in the past year, underperforming its sector and the S&P 500, due to concerns about fintech, acquisitions, and growth expectations. The company faces challenges like low return on invested capital, high debt, and increased competition, leading to a low valuation. A potential recovery hinges on strong execution, successful integration of acquisitions, improved profitability, and expanded strategic partnerships.

feed.title: Stryker (NYSE: SYK) CEO Kevin Lobo logs equity award vesting and tax share withholding
feed.summary: Stryker's (NYSE: SYK) CEO, Kevin Lobo, reported routine equity compensation activity. He exercised dividend-equivalent rights for 1,452 shares and had 26,492 shares withheld for tax obligations at a price of $335.67 per share. These transactions are standard for equity vesting and not indicative of open-market trading.

feed.title: Ross Stores (NASDAQ: ROST) CMO granted shares, delivers stock for taxes
feed.summary: Karen Fleming, President and Chief Merchandising Officer of Ross Dress for Less (Ross Stores), received a performance award of 12,868 shares and subsequently disposed of 9,931 shares to cover tax obligations. After these transactions, she directly holds 98,406.505 shares of Ross Stores common stock. The awarded shares will vest in installments between March 2026 and March 2028.

feed.title: Moderna stands as the best performing large-cap healthcare stock YTD (XLV:NYSEARCA)
feed.summary: Moderna (MRNA) is highlighted as the top-performing large-cap healthcare stock year-to-date in 2026. This article presents a list of the top 10 large-cap healthcare stocks with market capitalizations over $10 billion, ranked by their year-to-date performance, noting the sector's modest gains and defensive appeal.

feed.title: Synopsys to lay off 55 Sunnyvale employees as it funds Nvidia partnership
feed.summary: Synopsys is laying off 55 employees in Sunnyvale as part of a larger 10% workforce reduction announced in 2025. These cuts are intended to free up capital to support its strategic partnership with Nvidia and the integration of the recently acquired company Ansys. This follows an earlier round in January that eliminated 175 positions.

feed.title: C.H. Robinson Worldwide stock gains spotlight with Fast Company innovation nod and AI-driven restructuring amid freight headwinds
feed.summary: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (NASDAQ: CHRW) has been recognized by Fast Company for its Lean AI supply chain platform, which has improved on-time pickups by up to 35%. This innovation and a strategic restructuring effort, including workforce reductions and voluntary buyouts, are helping the company navigate a weak freight market. Despite revenue challenges, the company posted strong earnings and maintains a Moderate Buy consensus among analysts, with institutions like Contravisory showing increased interest.

feed.title: Blackstone plans to invest $15B in Japanese property over three years - report (BX:NYSE)
feed.summary: Blackstone (BX) intends to invest $15 billion in Japan's property market over the next three years. The private equity firm plans to acquire various types of properties including hotels, data centers, and logistics facilities. This move reflects Blackstone's strategy to expand its real estate portfolio in key international markets.

feed.title: William Blair, Jefferies Maintain LNTH Lantheus Holdings March 2026: Outperform Buy
feed.summary: William Blair and Jefferies both maintained their ratings on Lantheus Holdings (LNTH) on March 17, 2026, with William Blair reiterating Outperform and Jefferies reaffirming Buy while raising its price target to $110. These actions signal continued analyst confidence despite a recent -7.72% stock movement due to regulatory timing. Investors are advised to consider approval timing and execution risk, while Meyka AI rates LNTH with an 'A' grade.

feed.title: Insider Sales Shake Marathon Digital Shares
feed.summary: Marathon Digital (MARA) shares are down by 7.3% due to rising regulatory concerns, significant Q4 losses, and insider sales. The company reported revenues of $202.3M, missing forecasts, and a per-share loss of $4.52, signaling substantial financial underperformance. MARA is attempting to pivot into high-performance computing to reduce reliance on volatile bitcoin markets, but analysts remain cautious due to execution risks and a downgraded outlook.

feed.title: Ross Stores (ROST) operations chief granted 8,579 shares, 2,802 withheld
feed.summary: Ross Stores, Inc. President of Operations, Stephen C. Brinkley, was granted 8,579 shares of common stock as a performance award on March 20, 2026. A portion of these shares, 2,802, were immediately disposed of at $211.19 per share to cover tax obligations. Following these transactions, Brinkley directly holds 68,897 shares of Ross Stores common stock, with the granted shares vesting over three years.

feed.title: Robert Fishman Believes that The Walt Disney Company (DIS) Stock Needs Increased Confidence, Reports Bloomberg
feed.summary: A Bloomberg report, citing media analysts Robert Fishman and Rich Greenfield, suggests that The Walt Disney Company's stock needs increased investor confidence, particularly in its streaming growth and improved profit margins. The article highlights the challenges for Josh D'Amaro, Bob Iger's successor, in strengthening Disney's traditional media business and streaming subscriber growth amidst price hikes and declining traditional TV viewership. Greenfield further suggests separating ESPN and ABC networks to unlock shareholder value.

feed.title: 5 Most Profitable S&P 500 Stocks to Buy Now
feed.summary: This article identifies five of the most profitable S&P 500 stocks to consider buying now, with Edison International (NYSE:EIX) being the first company highlighted. Edison International recently received an upgraded price target from JPMorgan but a downgrade from Ladenburg due to potential earnings pressure. The company reported better-than-expected Q4 EPS and FY25 revenue, reflecting progress in grid hardening and wildfire mitigation efforts.

feed.title: AES website adds parent liquidity schedules with quarterly updates
feed.summary: The AES Corporation announced on March 24, 2026, the posting of new fixed income investor materials, "Parent Liquidity Schedules," on its website. This document, available under Investors > Fixed Income Resources & Debt Summary, will be updated quarterly to provide detailed information for bondholders. The release emphasizes transparency regarding the company’s liquidity and debt structure, following recent activities related to consent solicitations and a pending $15.00 per share cash merger.

feed.title: KB Home stock dips after Q1 earnings disappoint and guidance fails to buoy spirits
feed.summary: KB Home's stock declined following its first-quarter earnings report, which disappointed investors. Despite the earnings miss, the company's guidance for the upcoming period also failed to reassure the market, leading to a dip in share price.

feed.title: Ball Corp. stock faces pressure amid aluminum price volatility and beverage can demand shifts
feed.summary: Ball Corp. is experiencing pressure due to rising aluminum prices and moderating demand in the beverage industry, despite its strong position in sustainable aluminum packaging. The company's recent earnings were impacted by higher raw material costs, though management remains confident in future volume recovery and is implementing strategies like supply chain optimization and recycling investments. Strategic expansions and diversification into aerospace also support its long-term outlook, while US investors benefit from its strong domestic market presence and commitment to sustainability.

feed.title: Apollo Global Mourns Director David Simon’s Passing
feed.summary: Apollo Global Management announced the passing of independent director David Simon on March 22, 2026. Simon had served on the company's board since June 2021, and his death creates a vacancy that will require the company to review its board structure and governance. Analysts currently rate APO stock as a "Buy" with a $165.00 price target, and Spark, TipRanks' AI Analyst, rates it as "Outperform" due to solid financial performance and a bullish earnings outlook.

feed.title: John Dorfman: Defense stocks could explode upward again
feed.summary: John Dorfman predicts that military spending will increase in 2027 and 2028 due to ongoing geopolitical tensions, recommending investors include defense stocks in their portfolios. He provides a rundown of major U.S. and European defense contractors, highlighting their profitability, debt levels, and stock valuations. Dorfman also reviews his previous defense stock recommendations, noting their strong performance against the S&P 500.

feed.title: Form 424B2 GOLDMAN SACHS GROUP INC
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs Finance Corp. is offering $41,116,000 in Contingent Income Auto-Callable Securities linked to NVIDIA Corporation's stock, maturing in March 2029. These principal-at-risk securities offer quarterly contingent coupons if NVIDIA's stock price meets certain thresholds, with an automatic call feature if the stock price equals or exceeds the initial price. Investors risk losing a significant portion or all of their principal and do not participate in stock appreciation.

feed.title: Coca-Cola FEMSA Shareholders Approve 2025 Results and 2026 Dividend at Annual Meeting
feed.summary: Coca-Cola FEMSA's shareholders have approved the company's 2025 financial results and the composition of its governance bodies for 2026. They also authorized a cash dividend of Ps. 0.9675 per share, or Ps. 7.74 per KOF UBL unit, to be paid in four quarterly installments throughout 2026. Despite solid operating fundamentals, TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates KOF as Neutral due to cash-flow deterioration in 2025 and potential risks for 2026.

feed.title: FedEx launches two-hour and end-of-day delivery options
feed.summary: FedEx has launched a new expedited parcel service called "FedEx SameDay Local" offering two-hour and end-of-day delivery options, in collaboration with OneRail. This move comes shortly after Amazon introduced its own faster delivery options, including 1-hour and 3-hour services in select cities. The new FedEx service aims to provide consumers with more flexible delivery choices to meet varying needs, from immediate arrivals to more economical options.

feed.title: Stryker (NYSE: SYK) CFO withholds 204 shares for taxes
feed.summary: Stryker's CFO, Preston Wendell, reported a routine tax-related disposition where 204 shares of common stock were withheld to cover tax obligations. This non-market transaction was valued at $335.67 per share. Following this, Wendell directly holds 6,498 shares and indirectly holds 288 shares through a 401(k) plan, including shares from the ESPP and PIA plans.

feed.title: Liberty Global Schedules First Quarter 2026 Results Call
feed.summary: Liberty Global (LBTYA) will release its first-quarter 2026 results on May 1, 2026, followed by an investor call. The company, a major international provider of converged broadband, video, and mobile communications, recorded $21.6 billion in aggregate telecom revenues and a $3.4 billion investment portfolio at the end of 2025. Currently, analysts rate LBTYA as a Hold with a $13.00 price target, and TipRanks’ AI Analyst, Spark, assesses the stock as Neutral due to mixed financial performance and a generally positive but cautious earnings outlook.
